text.title: azure-front-door Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for azure-front-door. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Front Door Premium origins MUST use Private Link (`private_link` block in `azurerm_cdn_frontdoor_origin`) for production backends. Do NOT mix public and private origins in the same origin group — create separate origin groups for public vs private origins. Private Link connections MUST be approved on the target resource (App Service or App Gateway) before Front Door routes traffic to them.
rule.rationale: Without Private Link, traffic between Front Door PoP and the backend App Gateway or App Service transits the public internet. The docs imply private chaining but do not enforce it. A public-internet leg defeats the security model of Private Link and means the backend is reachable without Front Door WAF protection.

rule.statement: When Front Door is in front of Application Gateway, the App Gateway NSG MUST restrict inbound to the `AzureFrontDoor.Backend` service tag on ports 80 and 443. This prevents direct internet access that bypasses Front Door WAF. Add this NSG rule before pointing DNS at Front Door.
rule.rationale: If the App Gateway public IP is reachable directly from the internet, attackers bypass Front Door's WAF entirely. The `AzureFrontDoor.Backend` service tag covers all Front Door backend IPs and is maintained by Azure automatically.
